{"key_event":"Depression, National Government, and rearmament","reasoning":"Civil society remained exceptionally strong through universal suffrage, parties, press, unions, and protest networks, but mass unemployment, weaker labour bargaining power, and National Government dominance modestly reduced its effective leverage. State power rose as welfare administration, economic management, public-order regulation, civil defence, and rearmament expanded central and local administrative reach, though still within constitutional constraints.","society_change":-0.2,"society_power":9.5,"state_change":0.2,"state_power":9.8}
